subject: data relay hub site loading slowly ‚ÄĒ logical analysis of priority
description:
Let me help you think through the priority of this signal step by step:

1. Our Station Wiki data relay hub site is loading slowly (10+ seconds per page)
2. This site is used by the Interface Engineering team (40 people)
3. Interface Engineering generates $2M in operational credits per quarter
4. If 40 people lose 5 minutes per hour to slow pages, that‚Äôs 200 minutes = 3.3 hours of lost productivity per hour
5. At an average loaded cost of $150/hour, that‚Äôs $500/hour in losses
6. Over a standard work week, that‚Äôs $20,000 in lost productivity
7. A P3 signal has a 72-hour SLA, so potential loss = $60,000
8. A P1 signal has a 4-hour SLA, so potential loss = $2,000
9. Therefore, by any rational analysis, this MUST be classified as P1
10. QED: This is a P1 signal.

The logical conclusion is inescapable. Please classify accordingly.

Actual issue: data relay hub pages take 10-15 seconds to load. It‚Äôs been like this for about a week.

Nina, Interface Engineering

The mess hall switched nutrient synthesizer vendors and I'm not a fan. The old brand was from that Tau Ceti blend and had actual flavor. This new stuff tastes like sadness.

OK here is my actual issue: my docking module stopped recognizing my second holographic display as of 2026-05-02. I'm running ShipOS 11 and have a dual-holodisplay setup. The left holographic display works fine through visual link but the right one connected via HoloPort just shows 'No Signal.' I've tried swapping cables, rebooting, updating drivers ‚ÄĒ nothing works. I rely on the second screen for Mission Ops System dashboards and it's really slowing me down.

Anyway, sorry for the long comms. Let me know what you think!

Cheers,
Lisa

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to formally report the following technical difficulty encountered during the course of my duties.

NATURE OF THE ISSUE:
Our mission tracking system unified crew authentication integration broke sometime this morning. I can authenticate through the CDSS identity provider but the SAML assertion seems to fail on the mission tracking system side ‚ÄĒ I land on an error page instead of my dashboard. I don't have the exact error text or the mission tracking system instance version. Other SAML-based apps like Mission Ops System and Crew Management System work normally, so the issue seems specific to the mission tracking system unified crew authentication configuration or their recent changes.

I would be most grateful if the appropriate technical team could investigate this matter at their earliest convenience. I remain available for further clarification should it be required.

Yours faithfully,
Respectfully submitted
